items.text: Except what Allah, may will, He knows what is apparent and what is hidden.
items.secondaryText: إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ يَعْلَمُ ٱلْجَهْرَ وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ

items.text: And We shall make easy for you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) the easy way (i.e. the doing of righteous deeds).
items.secondaryText: وَنُيَسِّرُكَ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ

items.text: Therefore remind (men) in case the reminder profits (them).
items.secondaryText: فَذَكِّرْ إِن نَّفَعَتِ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ

items.text: The reminder will be received by him who fears (Allah),
items.secondaryText: سَيَذَّكَّرُ مَن يَخْشَىٰ

items.text: But it will be avoided by the wretched,
items.secondaryText: وَيَتَجَنَّبُهَا ٱلْأَشْقَى

items.text: Who will enter the great Fire and made to taste its burning,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى يَصْلَى ٱلنَّارَ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰ

items.text: Wherein he will neither die (to be in rest) nor live (a good living).
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَىٰ

items.text: Indeed whosoever purifies himself (by avoiding polytheism and accepting Islamic Monotheism) shall achieve success,
items.secondaryText: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ

items.text: And remembers (glorifies) the Name of his Lord (worships none but Allah), and prays (five compulsory prayers and Nawafil additional prayers).
items.secondaryText: وَذَكَرَ ٱسْمَ رَبِّهِۦ فَصَلَّىٰ

items.text: Nay, you prefer the life of this world;
items.secondaryText: بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا

items.text: Although the Hereafter is better and more lasting.
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْءَاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ

items.text: Verily! This is in the former Scriptures,
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَفِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ

items.text: The Scriptures of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Musa (Moses).
items.secondaryText: صُحُفِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ

items.text: Has there come to you the narration of the overwhelming (i.e. the Day of Resurrection);
items.secondaryText: هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ٱلْغَٰشِيَةِ

items.text: Some faces, that Day, will be humiliated (in the Hell-fire, i.e. the faces of all disbelievers, Jews and Christians, etc.).
items.secondaryText: وُجُوهٌۭ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَٰشِعَةٌ

items.text: Labouring (hard in the worldly life by worshipping others besides Allah), weary (in the Hereafter with humility and disgrace).
items.secondaryText: عَامِلَةٌۭ نَّاصِبَةٌۭ

items.text: They will enter in the hot blazing Fire,
items.secondaryText: تَصْلَىٰ نَارًا حَامِيَةًۭ

items.text: They will be given to drink from a boiling spring,
items.secondaryText: تُسْقَىٰ مِنْ عَيْنٍ ءَانِيَةٍۢ

items.text: No food will there be for them but a poisonous thorny plant,
items.secondaryText: لَّيْسَ لَهُمْ طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِن ضَرِيعٍۢ

items.text: Which will neither nourish nor avail against hunger.
items.secondaryText: لَّا يُسْمِنُ وَلَا يُغْنِى مِن جُوعٍۢ

items.text: (Other) faces, that Day, will be joyful,
items.secondaryText: وُجُوهٌۭ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ نَّاعِمَةٌۭ

items.text: Glad with their endeavour (for their good deeds which they did in this world, along with the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism).
items.secondaryText: لِّسَعْيِهَا رَاضِيَةٌۭ

items.text: In a lofty Paradise.
items.secondaryText: فِى جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَةٍۢ

items.text: Where they shall neither hear harmful speech nor falsehood,
items.secondaryText: لَّا تَسْمَعُ فِيهَا لَٰغِيَةًۭ

items.text: Therein will be a running spring,
items.secondaryText: فِيهَا عَيْنٌۭ جَارِيَةٌۭ

items.text: Therein will be thrones raised high,
items.secondaryText: فِيهَا سُرُرٌۭ مَّرْفُوعَةٌۭ

items.text: And cups set at hand.
items.secondaryText: وَأَكْوَابٌۭ مَّوْضُوعَةٌۭ

items.text: And cushions set in rows,
items.secondaryText: وَنَمَارِقُ مَصْفُوفَةٌۭ

items.text: And rich carpets (all) spread out.
items.secondaryText: وَزَرَابِىُّ مَبْثُوثَةٌ

items.text: Do they not look at the camels, how they are created?
items.secondaryText: أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى ٱلْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ

items.text: And at the heaven, how it is raised?
items.secondaryText: وَإِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ رُفِعَتْ

items.text: And at the mountains, how they are rooted and fixed firm?
items.secondaryText: وَإِلَى ٱلْجِبَالِ كَيْفَ نُصِبَتْ

items.text: And at the earth, how it is spread out?
items.secondaryText: وَإِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَيْفَ سُطِحَتْ

items.text: So remind them (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)), you are only a one who reminds.
items.secondaryText: فَذَكِّرْ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُذَكِّرٌۭ

items.text: You are not a dictator over them.
items.secondaryText: لَّسْتَ عَلَيْهِم بِمُصَيْطِرٍ

items.text: Save the one who turns away and disbelieves
items.secondaryText: إِلَّا مَن تَوَلَّىٰ وَكَفَرَ

items.text: Then Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.
items.secondaryText: فَيُعَذِّبُهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَكْبَرَ

items.text: Verily, to Us will be their return;
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ إِلَيْنَآ إِيَابَهُمْ

items.text: Then verily, for Us will be their reckoning.
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا حِسَابَهُم

items.text: By the dawn;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْفَجْرِ

items.text: By the ten nights (i.e. the first ten days of the month of Dhul-Hijjah).
items.secondaryText: وَلَيَالٍ عَشْرٍۢ

items.text: And by the even and the odd (of all the creations of Allah).
items.secondaryText: وَٱلشَّفْعِ وَٱلْوَتْرِ

items.text: And by the night when it departs.
items.secondaryText: وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا يَسْرِ

items.text: There is indeed in them (the above oaths) sufficient proofs for men of understanding (and that, they should avoid all kinds of sins and disbeliefs, etc.)!
items.secondaryText: هَلْ فِى ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٌۭ لِّذِى حِجْرٍ

items.text: Did you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) not see (thought) how your Lord dealt with 'Ad (people)?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ

items.text: Who were very tall like lofty pillars,
items.secondaryText: إِرَمَ ذَاتِ ٱلْعِمَادِ

items.text: The like of which were not created in the land?
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّتِى لَمْ يُخْلَقْ مِثْلُهَا فِى ٱلْبِلَٰدِ

items.text: And (with) Thamud (people), who cut (hewed) out rocks in the valley (to make dwellings)?
items.secondaryText: وَثَمُودَ ٱلَّذِينَ جَابُوا۟ ٱلصَّخْرَ بِٱلْوَادِ

items.text: And (with) Fir'aun (Pharaoh), who had pegs (who used to torture men by binding them to pegs)?
items.secondaryText: وَفِرْعَوْنَ ذِى ٱلْأَوْتَادِ

items.text: Who did transgress beyond bounds in the lands (in the disobedience of Allah).
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِينَ طَغَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْبِلَٰدِ

items.text: And made therein much mischief.
items.secondaryText: فَأَكْثَرُوا۟ فِيهَا ٱلْفَسَادَ

items.text: So your Lord poured on them different kinds of severe torment.
items.secondaryText: فَصَبَّ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّكَ سَوْطَ عَذَابٍ

items.text: Verily, your Lord is Ever Watchful (over them).
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِٱلْمِرْصَادِ

items.text: As for man, when his Lord tries him by giving him honour and gifts, then he says (puffed up): "My Lord has honoured me."
items.secondaryText: فَأَمَّا ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ إِذَا مَا ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَأَكْرَمَهُۥ وَنَعَّمَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّىٓ أَكْرَمَنِ

items.text: But when He tries him, by straitening his means of life, he says: "My Lord has humiliated me!"
items.secondaryText: وَأَمَّآ إِذَا مَا ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّىٓ أَهَٰنَنِ

items.text: Nay! But you treat not the orphans with kindness and generosity (i.e. you neither treat them well, nor give them their exact right of inheritance)!
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا ۖ بَل لَّا تُكْرِمُونَ ٱلْيَتِيمَ

items.text: And urge not on the feeding of AlMiskin (the poor)!
items.secondaryText: وَلَا تَحَٰٓضُّونَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ

items.text: And you devour inheritance all with greed,
items.secondaryText: وَتَأْكُلُونَ ٱلتُّرَاثَ أَكْلًۭا لَّمًّۭا

items.text: And you love wealth with much love!
items.secondaryText: وَتُحِبُّونَ ٱلْمَالَ حُبًّۭا جَمًّۭا

items.text: Nay! When the earth is ground to powder,
items.secondaryText: كَلَّآ إِذَا دُكَّتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ دَكًّۭا دَكًّۭا

items.text: And your Lord comes with the angels in rows,
items.secondaryText: وَجَآءَ رَبُّكَ وَٱلْمَلَكُ صَفًّۭا صَفًّۭا

items.text: And Hell will be brought near that Day. On that Day will man remember, but how will that remembrance (then) avail him?
items.secondaryText: وَجِا۟ىٓءَ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِجَهَنَّمَ ۚ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ يَتَذَكَّرُ ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ

items.text: He will say: "Alas! Would that I had sent forth (good deeds) for (this) my life!"
items.secondaryText: يَقُولُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى قَدَّمْتُ لِحَيَاتِى

items.text: So on that Day, none will punish as He will punish.
items.secondaryText: فَيَوْمَئِذٍۢ لَّا يُعَذِّبُ عَذَابَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌۭ

items.text: And none will bind as He will bind.
items.secondaryText: وَلَا يُوثِقُ وَثَاقَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌۭ

items.text: (It will be said to the pious): "O (you) the one in (complete) rest and satisfaction!
items.secondaryText: يَٰٓأَيَّتُهَا ٱلنَّفْسُ ٱلْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ

items.text: "Come back to your Lord, Well-pleased (yourself) and well-pleasing unto Him!
items.secondaryText: ٱرْجِعِىٓ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةًۭ مَّرْضِيَّةًۭ

items.text: "Enter you, then, among My honoured slaves,
items.secondaryText: فَٱدْخُلِى فِى عِبَٰدِى

items.text: "And enter you My Paradise!"
items.secondaryText: وَٱدْخُلِى جَنَّتِى

items.text: I swear by this city (Makkah);
items.secondaryText: لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِهَٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ

items.text: And you are free (from sin, to punish the enemies of Islam on the Day of the conquest) in this city (Makkah).
items.secondaryText: وَأَنتَ حِلٌّۢ بِهَٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ

items.text: And by the begetter (i.e. Adam) and that which he begot (i.e. his progeny);
items.secondaryText: وَوَالِدٍۢ وَمَا وَلَدَ

items.text: Verily, We have created man in toil.
items.secondaryText: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ فِى كَبَدٍ

items.text: Thinks he that none can overcome him?
items.secondaryText: أَيَحْسَبُ أَن لَّن يَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَحَدٌۭ

items.text: He says (boastfully): "I have wasted wealth in abundance!"
items.secondaryText: يَقُولُ أَهْلَكْتُ مَالًۭا لُّبَدًا

items.text: Thinks he that none sees him?
items.secondaryText: أَيَحْسَبُ أَن لَّمْ يَرَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌ

items.text: Have We not made for him a pair of eyes?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُۥ عَيْنَيْنِ

items.text: And a tongue and a pair of lips?
items.secondaryText: وَلِسَانًۭا وَشَفَتَيْنِ

items.text: And shown him the two ways (good and evil)?
items.secondaryText: وَهَدَيْنَٰهُ ٱلنَّجْدَيْنِ

items.text: But he has made no effort to pass on the path that is steep.
items.secondaryText: فَلَا ٱقْتَحَمَ ٱلْعَقَبَةَ

items.text: And what will make you know the path that is steep?
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْعَقَبَةُ

items.text: (It is) Freeing a neck (slave, etc.)
items.secondaryText: فَكُّ رَقَبَةٍ

items.text: Or giving food in a day of hunger (famine),
items.secondaryText: أَوْ إِطْعَٰمٌۭ فِى يَوْمٍۢ ذِى مَسْغَبَةٍۢ

items.text: To an orphan near of kin.
items.secondaryText: يَتِيمًۭا ذَا مَقْرَبَةٍ

items.text: Or to a Miskin (poor) afflicted with misery.
items.secondaryText: أَوْ مِسْكِينًۭا ذَا مَتْرَبَةٍۢ

items.text: Then he became one of those who believed, and recommended one another to perseverance and patience, and (also) recommended one another to pity and compassion.
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْمَرْحَمَةِ

items.text: They are those on the Right Hand (the dwellers of Paradise),
items.secondaryText: أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَٰبُ ٱلْمَيْمَنَةِ

items.text: But those who disbelieved in Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), they are those on the Left Hand (the dwellers of Hell).
items.secondaryText: وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا هُمْ أَصْحَٰبُ ٱلْمَشْـَٔمَةِ

items.text: The Fire will be shut over them (i.e. they will be enveloped by the Fire without any opening or window or outlet.
items.secondaryText: عَلَيْهِمْ نَارٌۭ مُّؤْصَدَةٌۢ

items.text: And by the sun and its brightness;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلشَّمْسِ وَضُحَىٰهَا

items.text: And by the moon as it follows it (the sun);
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْقَمَرِ إِذَا تَلَىٰهَا

items.text: And by the day as it shows up (the sun's) brightness;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلنَّهَارِ إِذَا جَلَّىٰهَا

items.text: And by the night as it conceals it (the sun);
items.secondaryText: وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَىٰهَا

items.text: And by the heaven and Him Who built it;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا بَنَىٰهَا

items.text: And by the earth and Him Who spread it,
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا طَحَىٰهَا

items.text: And by Nafs (Adam or a person or a soul, etc.), and Him Who perfected him in proportion;
items.secondaryText: وَنَفْسٍۢ وَمَا سَوَّىٰهَا

items.text: Then He showed him what is wrong for him and what is right for him;
items.secondaryText: فَأَلْهَمَهَا فُجُورَهَا وَتَقْوَىٰهَا

items.text: Indeed he succeeds who purifies his ownself (i.e. obeys and performs all that Allah ordered, by following the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism and by doing righteous good deeds).
items.secondaryText: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَن زَكَّىٰهَا

items.text: And indeed he fails who corrupts his ownself (i.e. disobeys what Allah has ordered by rejecting the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism or by following polytheism, etc. or by doing every kind of evil wicked deeds).
items.secondaryText: وَقَدْ خَابَ مَن دَسَّىٰهَا

items.text: Thamud (people) denied (their Prophet) through their transgression (by rejecting the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism, and by following polytheism, and by committing every kind of sin).
items.secondaryText: كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِطَغْوَىٰهَآ

items.text: When the most wicked man among them went forth (to kill the she-camel).
items.secondaryText: إِذِ ٱنۢبَعَثَ أَشْقَىٰهَا

items.text: But the Messenger of Allah [Salih (Saleh)] said to them: "Be cautious! Fear the evil end. That is the she-camel of Allah! (Do not harm it) and bar it not from having its drink!"
items.secondaryText: فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ نَاقَةَ ٱللَّهِ وَسُقْيَٰهَا

items.text: Then they denied him and they killed it. So their Lord destroyed them because of their sin, and made them equal in destruction (i.e. all grades of people, rich and poor, strong and weak, etc.)!
items.secondaryText: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِذَنۢبِهِمْ فَسَوَّىٰهَا

items.text: And He (Allah) feared not the consequences thereof.
items.secondaryText: وَلَا يَخَافُ عُقْبَٰهَا

items.text: By the night as it envelops;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا يَغْشَىٰ

items.text: And by the day as it appears in brightness;
items.secondaryText: وَٱلنَّهَارِ إِذَا تَجَلَّىٰ

items.text: And by Him Who created male and female;
items.secondaryText: وَمَا خَلَقَ ٱلذَّكَرَ وَٱلْأُنثَىٰٓ

items.text: Certainly, your efforts and deeds are diverse (different in aims and purposes);
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ سَعْيَكُمْ لَشَتَّىٰ

items.text: As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah and fears Him,
items.secondaryText: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ وَٱتَّقَىٰ

items.text: And believes in Al-Husna.
items.secondaryText: وَصَدَّقَ بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ

items.text: We will make smooth for him the path of ease (goodness).
items.secondaryText: فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُۥ لِلْيُسْرَىٰ

items.text: But he who is greedy miser and thinks himself self-sufficient.
items.secondaryText: وَأَمَّا مَنۢ بَخِلَ وَٱسْتَغْنَىٰ

items.text: And gives the lie to Al-Husna (see Verse No: 6 footnote);
items.secondaryText: وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ

items.text: We will make smooth for him the path for evil;
items.secondaryText: فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُۥ لِلْعُسْرَىٰ

items.text: And what will his wealth benefit him when he goes down (in destruction).
items.secondaryText: وَمَا يُغْنِى عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥٓ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰٓ

items.text: Truly! Ours it is (to give) guidance,
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا لَلْهُدَىٰ

items.text: And truly, unto Us (belong) the last (Hereafter) and the first (this world).
items.secondaryText: وَإِنَّ لَنَا لَلْءَاخِرَةَ وَٱلْأُولَىٰ

items.text: Therefore I have warned you of a Fire blazing fiercely (Hell);
items.secondaryText: فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ نَارًۭا تَلَظَّىٰ

items.text: None shall enter it save the most wretched,
items.secondaryText: لَا يَصْلَىٰهَآ إِلَّا ٱلْأَشْقَى

items.text: Who denies and turns away.
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ

items.text: And Al-Muttaqun (the pious and righteous - see V. 2:2) will be far removed from it (Hell).
items.secondaryText: وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا ٱلْأَتْقَى

items.text: He who spends his wealth for increase in self-purification,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى يُؤْتِى مَالَهُۥ يَتَزَكَّىٰ

items.text: And have in his mind no favour from anyone for which a reward is expected in return,
items.secondaryText: وَمَا لِأَحَدٍ عِندَهُۥ مِن نِّعْمَةٍۢ تُجْزَىٰٓ

items.text: Except only the desire to seek the Countenance of his Lord, the Most High;
items.secondaryText: إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ

items.text: He surely will be pleased (when he will enter Paradise).
items.secondaryText: وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَىٰ

items.text: By the forenoon (after sun-rise);
items.secondaryText: وَٱلضُّحَىٰ

items.text: And by the night when it is still (or darkens);
items.secondaryText: وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا سَجَىٰ

items.text: Your Lord (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) has neither forsaken you nor hated you.
items.secondaryText: مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ

items.text: And indeed the Hereafter is better for you than the present (life of this world).
items.secondaryText: وَلَلْءَاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌۭ لَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ

items.text: And verily, your Lord will give you (all i.e. good) so that you shall be well-pleased.
items.secondaryText: وَلَسَوْفَ يُعْطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرْضَىٰٓ

items.text: Did He not find you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) an orphan and gave you a refuge?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيمًۭا فَـَٔاوَىٰ

items.text: And He found you unaware (of the Quran, its legal laws, and Prophethood, etc.) and guided you?
items.secondaryText: وَوَجَدَكَ ضَآلًّۭا فَهَدَىٰ

items.text: And He found you poor, and made you rich (selfsufficient with selfcontentment, etc.)?
items.secondaryText: وَوَجَدَكَ عَآئِلًۭا فَأَغْنَىٰ

items.text: Therefore, treat not the orphan with oppression,
items.secondaryText: فَأَمَّا ٱلْيَتِيمَ فَلَا تَقْهَرْ

items.text: And repulse not the beggar;
items.secondaryText: وَأَمَّا ٱلسَّآئِلَ فَلَا تَنْهَرْ

items.text: And proclaim the Grace of your Lord (i.e. the Prophethood and all other Graces).
items.secondaryText: وَأَمَّا بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّثْ

items.text: Have We not opened your breast for you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him))?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ لَكَ صَدْرَكَ

items.text: And removed from you your burden,
items.secondaryText: وَوَضَعْنَا عَنكَ وِزْرَكَ

items.text: Which weighed down your back?
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنقَضَ ظَهْرَكَ

items.text: And raised high your fame?
items.secondaryText: وَرَفَعْنَا لَكَ ذِكْرَكَ

items.text: So verily, with the hardship, there is relief,
items.secondaryText: فَإِنَّ مَعَ ٱلْعُسْرِ يُسْرًا

items.text: Verily, with the hardship, there is relief (i.e. there is one hardship with two reliefs, so one hardship cannot overcome two reliefs).
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ مَعَ ٱلْعُسْرِ يُسْرًۭا

items.text: So when you have finished (from your occupation), then stand up for Allah's worship (i.e. stand up for prayer).
items.secondaryText: فَإِذَا فَرَغْتَ فَٱنصَبْ

items.text: And to your Lord (Alone) turn (all your intentions and hopes and) your invocations.
items.secondaryText: وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَٱرْغَب

items.text: By the fig, and the olive,
items.secondaryText: بِّسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ وَٱلتِّينِ وَٱلزَّيْتُونِ

items.text: By Mount Sinai,
items.secondaryText: وَطُورِ سِينِينَ

items.text: And by this city of security (Makkah).
items.secondaryText: وَهَٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدِ ٱلْأَمِينِ

items.text: Verily, We created man of the best stature (mould),
items.secondaryText: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ فِىٓ أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍۢ

items.text: Then We reduced him to the lowest of the low,
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَٰهُ أَسْفَلَ سَٰفِلِينَ

items.text: Save those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous deeds, then they shall have a reward without end (Paradise).
items.secondaryText: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍۢ

items.text: Then what (or who) causes you (O disbelievers) to deny the Recompense (i.e. Day of Resurrection)?
items.secondaryText: فَمَا يُكَذِّبُكَ بَعْدُ بِٱلدِّينِ

items.text: Is not Allah the Best of judges?
items.secondaryText: أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَحْكَمِ ٱلْحَٰكِمِينَ

items.text: Read! In the Name of your Lord, Who has created (all that exists),
items.secondaryText: ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ

items.text: Has created man from a clot (a piece of thick coagulated blood).
items.secondaryText: خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ

items.text: Read! And your Lord is the Most Generous,
items.secondaryText: ٱقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ ٱلْأَكْرَمُ

items.text: Who has taught (the writing) by the pen [the first person to write was Prophet Idrees (Enoch)].
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَ بِٱلْقَلَمِ

items.text: Has taught man that which he knew not.
items.secondaryText: عَلَّمَ ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ مَا لَمْ يَعْلَمْ

items.text: Nay! Verily, man does transgress all bounds (in disbelief and evil deed, etc.).
items.secondaryText: كَلَّآ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ لَيَطْغَىٰٓ

items.text: Because he considers himself self-sufficient.
items.secondaryText: أَن رَّءَاهُ ٱسْتَغْنَىٰٓ

items.text: Surely! Unto your Lord is the return.
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ٱلرُّجْعَىٰٓ

items.text: Have you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) seen him (i.e. Abu Jahl) who prevents,
items.secondaryText: أَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى يَنْهَىٰ

items.text: A slave (Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) when he prays?
items.secondaryText: عَبْدًا إِذَا صَلَّىٰٓ

items.text: Tell me, if he (Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) is on the guidance (of Allah)?
items.secondaryText: أَرَءَيْتَ إِن كَانَ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ

items.text: Or enjoins piety?
items.secondaryText: أَوْ أَمَرَ بِٱلتَّقْوَىٰٓ

items.text: Tell me if he (the disbeliever, Abu Jahl) denies (the truth, i.e. this Quran), and turns away?
items.secondaryText: أَرَءَيْتَ إِن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ

items.text: Knows he not that Allah does see (what he does)?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ يَعْلَم بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَرَىٰ

items.text: Nay! If he (Abu Jahl) ceases not, We will catch him by the forelock,
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا لَئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ لَنَسْفَعًۢا بِٱلنَّاصِيَةِ

items.text: A lying, sinful forelock!
items.secondaryText: نَاصِيَةٍۢ كَٰذِبَةٍ خَاطِئَةٍۢ

items.text: Then, let him call upon his council (of helpers),
items.secondaryText: فَلْيَدْعُ نَادِيَهُۥ

items.text: We will call the guards of Hell (to deal with him)!
items.secondaryText: سَنَدْعُ ٱلزَّبَانِيَةَ

items.text: Nay! (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him))! Do not obey him (Abu Jahl). Fall prostrate and draw near to Allah!
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا لَا تُطِعْهُ وَٱسْجُدْ وَٱقْتَرِب ۩

items.text: Verily! We have sent it (this Quran) down in the night of Al-Qadr (Decree)
items.secondaryText: بِّسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَٰهُ فِى لَيْلَةِ ٱلْقَدْرِ

items.text: And what will make you know what the night of Al-Qadr (Decree) is?
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا لَيْلَةُ ٱلْقَدْرِ

items.text: The night of Al-Qadr (Decree) is better than a thousand months (i.e. worshipping Allah in that night is better than worshipping Him a thousand months, (i.e. 83 years and 4 months).
items.secondaryText: لَيْلَةُ ٱلْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌۭ مِّنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍۢ

items.text: Therein descend the angels and the Ruh [Jibrael (Gabriel)] by Allah's Permission with all Decrees,
items.secondaryText: تَنَزَّلُ ٱلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمْرٍۢ

items.text: Peace! (All that night, there is Peace and Goodness from Allah to His believing slaves) until the appearance of dawn.
items.secondaryText: سَلَٰمٌ هِىَ حَتَّىٰ مَطْلَعِ ٱلْفَجْرِ

items.text: Those who disbelieve from among the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) and among Al-Mushrikun, were not going to leave (their disbelief) until there came to them clear evidence.
items.secondaryText: لَمْ يَكُنِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَٰبِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنفَكِّينَ حَتَّىٰ تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَةُ

items.text: A Messenger (Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) from Allah, reciting (the Quran) purified pages [purified from Al-Batil (falsehood, etc.)].
items.secondaryText: رَسُولٌۭ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ يَتْلُوا۟ صُحُفًۭا مُّطَهَّرَةًۭ

items.text: Containing correct and straight laws from Allah.
items.secondaryText: فِيهَا كُتُبٌۭ قَيِّمَةٌۭ

items.text: And the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) differed not until after there came to them clear evidence. (i.e. Prophet Muhammad (Peace be upon him) and whatever was revealed to him).
items.secondaryText: وَمَا تَفَرَّقَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَٰبَ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَةُ

items.text: And they were commanded not, but that they should worship Allah, and worship none but Him Alone (abstaining from ascribing partners to Him), and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and give Zakat: and that is the right religion.
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أُمِرُوٓا۟ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ حُنَفَآءَ وَيُقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ ٱلْقَيِّمَةِ

items.text: Verily, those who disbelieve (in the religion of Islam, the Quran and Prophet Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) from among the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) and Al-Mushrikun will abide in the Fire of Hell. They are the worst of creatures.
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَٰبِ وَٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ فِى نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ ۚ أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ هُمْ شَرُّ ٱلْبَرِيَّةِ

items.text: Verily, those who believe [in the Oneness of Allah, and in His Messenger Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) including all obligations ordered by Islam] and do righteous good deeds, they are the best of creatures.
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ ٱلْبَرِيَّةِ

items.text: Their reward with their Lord is 'Adn (Eden) Paradise (Gardens of Eternity), underneath which rivers flow, they will abide therein forever, Allah Well-Pleased with them, and they with Him. That is for him who fears his Lord.
items.secondaryText: جَزَآؤُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّٰتُ عَدْنٍۢ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًۭا ۖ رَّضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِىَ رَبَّهُۥ

items.text: When the earth is shaken with its (final) earthquake.
items.secondaryText: إِذَا زُلْزِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ زِلْزَالَهَا

items.text: And when the earth throws out its burdens,
items.secondaryText: وَأَخْرَجَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ أَثْقَالَهَا

items.text: And man will say: "What is the matter with it?"
items.secondaryText: وَقَالَ ٱلْإِنسَٰنُ مَا لَهَا

items.text: That Day it will declare its information (about all what happened over it of good or evil).
items.secondaryText: يَوْمَئِذٍۢ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا

items.text: Because your Lord has inspired it.
items.secondaryText: بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوْحَىٰ لَهَا

items.text: That Day mankind will proceed in scattered groups that they may be shown their deeds.
items.secondaryText: يَوْمَئِذٍۢ يَصْدُرُ ٱلنَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًۭا لِّيُرَوْا۟ أَعْمَٰلَهُمْ

items.text: So whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it.
items.secondaryText: فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًۭا يَرَهُۥ

items.text: And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it.
items.secondaryText: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍۢ شَرًّۭا يَرَهُۥ

items.text: By the (steeds) that run, with panting (breath),
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْعَٰدِيَٰتِ ضَبْحًۭا

items.text: Striking sparks of fire (by their hooves),
items.secondaryText: فَٱلْمُورِيَٰتِ قَدْحًۭا

items.text: And scouring to the raid at dawn
items.secondaryText: فَٱلْمُغِيرَٰتِ صُبْحًۭا

items.text: And raise the dust in clouds the while,
items.secondaryText: فَأَثَرْنَ بِهِۦ نَقْعًۭا

items.text: Penetrating forthwith as one into the midst (of the foe);
items.secondaryText: فَوَسَطْنَ بِهِۦ جَمْعًا

items.text: Verily! Man (disbeliever) is ungrateful to his Lord;
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ لِرَبِّهِۦ لَكَنُودٌۭ

items.text: And to that fact he bears witness (by his deeds);
items.secondaryText: وَإِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌۭ

items.text: And verily, he is violent in the love of wealth.
items.secondaryText: وَإِنَّهُۥ لِحُبِّ ٱلْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ

items.text: Knows he not that when the contents of the graves are brought out and poured forth (all mankind is resurrected).
items.secondaryText: ۞ أَفَلَا يَعْلَمُ إِذَا بُعْثِرَ مَا فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ

items.text: And that which is in the breasts (of men) shall be made known.
items.secondaryText: وَحُصِّلَ مَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ

items.text: Verily, that Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) their Lord will be Well-Acquainted with them (as to their deeds), (and will reward them for their deeds).
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ رَبَّهُم بِهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ لَّخَبِيرٌۢ

items.text: Al-Qari'ah (the striking Hour i.e. the Day of Resurrection),
items.secondaryText: ٱلْقَارِعَةُ

items.text: What is the striking (Hour)?
items.secondaryText: مَا ٱلْقَارِعَةُ

items.text: And what will make you know what the striking (Hour) is?
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْقَارِعَةُ

items.text: It is a Day whereon mankind will be like moths scattered about,
items.secondaryText: يَوْمَ يَكُونُ ٱلنَّاسُ كَٱلْفَرَاشِ ٱلْمَبْثُوثِ

items.text: And the mountains will be like carded wool,
items.secondaryText: وَتَكُونُ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَٱلْعِهْنِ ٱلْمَنفُوشِ

items.text: Then as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be heavy,
items.secondaryText: فَأَمَّا مَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ

items.text: He will live a pleasant life (in Paradise).
items.secondaryText: فَهُوَ فِى عِيشَةٍۢ رَّاضِيَةٍۢ

items.text: But as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be light,
items.secondaryText: وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ

items.text: He will have his home in Hawiyah (pit, i.e. Hell).
items.secondaryText: فَأُمُّهُۥ هَاوِيَةٌۭ

items.text: And what will make you know what it is?
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا هِيَهْ

items.text: (It is) a hot blazing Fire!
items.secondaryText: نَارٌ حَامِيَةٌۢ

items.text: The mutual rivalry for piling up of worldly things diverts you,
items.secondaryText: أَلْهَىٰكُمُ ٱلتَّكَاثُرُ

items.text: Until you visit the graves (i.e. till you die).
items.secondaryText: حَتَّىٰ زُرْتُمُ ٱلْمَقَابِرَ

items.text: Nay! You shall come to know!
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ

items.text: Again, Nay! You shall come to know!
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ

items.text: Nay! If you knew with a sure knowledge (the end result of piling up, you would not have occupied yourselves in worldly things)
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عِلْمَ ٱلْيَقِينِ

items.text: Verily, You shall see the blazing Fire (Hell)!
items.secondaryText: لَتَرَوُنَّ ٱلْجَحِيمَ

items.text: And again, you shall see it with certainty of sight!
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ لَتَرَوُنَّهَا عَيْنَ ٱلْيَقِينِ

items.text: Then, on that Day, you shall be asked about the delight (you indulged in, in this world)!
items.secondaryText: ثُمَّ لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَنِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ

items.text: By Al-'Asr (the time).
items.secondaryText: وَٱلْعَصْرِ

items.text: Verily! Man is in loss,
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَٰنَ لَفِى خُسْرٍ

items.text: Except those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, and recommend one another to the truth (i.e. order one another to perform all kinds of good deeds (Al-Ma'ruf) which Allah has ordained, and abstain from all kinds of sins and evil deeds (Al-Munkar) which Allah has forbidden), and recommend one another to patience (for the sufferings, harms, and injuries which one may encounter in Allah's Cause during preaching His religion of Islamic Monotheism or Jihad, etc.).
items.secondaryText: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ

items.text: Woe to every slanderer and backbiter.
items.secondaryText: وَيْلٌۭ لِّكُلِّ هُمَزَةٍۢ لُّمَزَةٍ

items.text: Who has gathered wealth and counted it,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى جَمَعَ مَالًۭا وَعَدَّدَهُۥ

items.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him last forever!
items.secondaryText: يَحْسَبُ أَنَّ مَالَهُۥٓ أَخْلَدَهُۥ

items.text: Nay! Verily, he will be thrown into the crushing Fire.
items.secondaryText: كَلَّا ۖ لَيُنۢبَذَنَّ فِى ٱلْحُطَمَةِ

items.text: And what will make you know what the crushing Fire is?
items.secondaryText: وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلْحُطَمَةُ

items.text: The fire of Allah, kindled,
items.secondaryText: نَارُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُوقَدَةُ

items.text: Which leaps up over the hearts,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّتِى تَطَّلِعُ عَلَى ٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةِ

items.text: Verily, it shall be closed in on them,
items.secondaryText: إِنَّهَا عَلَيْهِم مُّؤْصَدَةٌۭ

items.text: In pillars stretched forth (i.e. they will be punished in the Fire with pillars, etc.).
items.secondaryText: فِى عَمَدٍۢ مُّمَدَّدَةٍۭ

items.text: Have you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) not seen how your Lord dealt with the Owners of the Elephant? [The elephant army which came from Yemen under the command of Abrahah Al-Ashram intending to destroy the Ka'bah at Makkah].
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصْحَٰبِ ٱلْفِيلِ

items.text: Did He not make their plot go astray?
items.secondaryText: أَلَمْ يَجْعَلْ كَيْدَهُمْ فِى تَضْلِيلٍۢ

items.text: And sent against them birds, in flocks,
items.secondaryText: وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا أَبَابِيلَ

items.text: Striking them with stones of Sijjil.
items.secondaryText: تَرْمِيهِم بِحِجَارَةٍۢ مِّن سِجِّيلٍۢ

items.text: And made them like an empty field of stalks (of which the corn has been eaten up by cattle).
items.secondaryText: فَجَعَلَهُمْ كَعَصْفٍۢ مَّأْكُولٍۭ

items.text: (It is a great Grace and Protection from Allah), for the taming of the Quraish,
items.secondaryText: لِإِيلَٰفِ قُرَيْشٍ

items.text: (And with all those Allah's Grace and Protections for their taming, We cause) the (Quraish) caravans to set forth safe in winter (to the south), and in summer (to the north without any fear),
items.secondaryText: إِۦلَٰفِهِمْ رِحْلَةَ ٱلشِّتَآءِ وَٱلصَّيْفِ

items.text: So let them worship (Allah) the Lord of this House (the Ka'bah in Makkah).
items.secondaryText: فَلْيَعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّ هَٰذَا ٱلْبَيْتِ

items.text: (He) Who has fed them against hunger, and has made them safe from fear.
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْعَمَهُم مِّن جُوعٍۢ وَءَامَنَهُم مِّنْ خَوْفٍۭ

items.text: Have you seen him who denies the Recompense?
items.secondaryText: أَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى يُكَذِّبُ بِٱلدِّينِ

items.text: That is he who repulses the orphan (harshly),
items.secondaryText: فَذَٰلِكَ ٱلَّذِى يَدُعُّ ٱلْيَتِيمَ

items.text: And urges not the feeding of AlMiskin (the poor),
items.secondaryText: وَلَا يَحُضُّ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ

items.text: So woe unto those performers of Salat (prayers) (hypocrites),
items.secondaryText: فَوَيْلٌۭ لِّلْمُصَلِّينَ

items.text: Who delay their Salat (prayer) from their stated fixed times,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَن صَلَاتِهِمْ سَاهُونَ

items.text: Those who do good deeds only to be seen (of men),
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ يُرَآءُونَ

items.text: And refuse Al-Ma'un (small kindnesses e.g. salt, sugar, water, etc.).
items.secondaryText: وَيَمْنَعُونَ ٱلْمَاعُونَ

items.text: Verily, We have granted you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) Al-Kauthar (a river in Paradise);
items.secondaryText: إِنَّآ أَعْطَيْنَٰكَ ٱلْكَوْثَرَ

items.text: Therefore turn in prayer to your Lord and sacrifice (to Him only).
items.secondaryText: فَصَلِّ لِرَبِّكَ وَٱنْحَرْ

items.text: For he who makes you angry (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)), - he will be cut off (from every good thing in this world and in the Hereafter).
items.secondaryText: إِنَّ شَانِئَكَ هُوَ ٱلْأَبْتَرُ

items.text: Say (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him) to these Mushrikun and Kafirun): "O Al-Kafirun (disbelievers in Allah, in His Oneness, in His Angels, in His Books, in His Messengers, in the Day of Resurrection, and in Al-Qadar, etc.)!
items.secondaryText: قُلْ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْكَٰفِرُونَ

items.text: "I worship not that which you worship,
items.secondaryText: لَآ أَعْبُدُ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ

items.text: "Nor will you worship that which I worship.
items.secondaryText: وَلَآ أَنتُمْ عَٰبِدُونَ مَآ أَعْبُدُ

items.text: "And I shall not worship that which you are worshipping.
items.secondaryText: وَلَآ أَنَا۠ عَابِدٌۭ مَّا عَبَدتُّمْ

items.text: "Nor will you worship that which I worship.
items.secondaryText: وَلَآ أَنتُمْ عَٰبِدُونَ مَآ أَعْبُدُ

items.text: "To you be your religion, and to me my religion (Islamic Monotheism)."
items.secondaryText: لَكُمْ دِينُكُمْ وَلِىَ دِينِ

items.text: When comes the Help of Allah (to you, O Muhammad (Peace be upon him) against your enemies) and the conquest (of Makkah),
items.secondaryText: إِذَا جَآءَ نَصْرُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْفَتْحُ

items.text: And you see that the people enter Allah's religion (Islam) in crowds,
items.secondaryText: وَرَأَيْتَ ٱلنَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِى دِينِ ٱللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًۭا

items.text: So glorify the Praises of your Lord, and ask for His Forgiveness. Verily, He is the One Who accepts the repentance and forgives.
items.secondaryText: فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ تَوَّابًۢا

items.text: Perish the two hands of Abu Lahab (an uncle of the Prophet), and perish he!
items.secondaryText: تَبَّتْ يَدَآ أَبِى لَهَبٍۢ وَتَبَّ

items.text: His wealth and his children (etc.) will not benefit him!
items.secondaryText: مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَمَا كَسَبَ

items.text: He will be burnt in a Fire of blazing flames!
items.secondaryText: سَيَصْلَىٰ نَارًۭا ذَاتَ لَهَبٍۢ

items.text: And his wife too, who carries wood (thorns of Sadan which she used to put on the way of the Prophet (Peace be upon him), or use to slander him).
items.secondaryText: وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ حَمَّالَةَ ٱلْحَطَبِ

items.text: In her neck is a twisted rope of Masad (palm fibre).
items.secondaryText: فِى جِيدِهَا حَبْلٌۭ مِّن مَّسَدٍۭ

items.text: Say (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)): "He is Allah, (the) One.
items.secondaryText: قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ

items.text: "Allah-us-Samad (The Self-Sufficient Master, Whom all creatures need, He neither eats nor drinks).
items.secondaryText: ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ

items.text: "He begets not, nor was He begotten;
items.secondaryText: لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ

items.text: "And there is none co-equal or comparable unto Him."
items.secondaryText: وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ

items.text: Say: "I seek refuge with (Allah) the Lord of the daybreak,
items.secondaryText: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْفَلَقِ

items.text: "From the evil of what He has created;
items.secondaryText: مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ

items.text: "And from the evil of the darkening (night) as it comes with its darkness; (or the moon as it sets or goes away).
items.secondaryText: وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ

items.text: "And from the evil of the witchcrafts when they blow in the knots,
items.secondaryText: وَمِن شَرِّ ٱلنَّفَّٰثَٰتِ فِى ٱلْعُقَدِ

items.text: "And from the evil of the envier when he envies."
items.secondaryText: وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ

items.text: Say: "I seek refuge with (Allah) the Lord of mankind,
items.secondaryText: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ

items.text: "The King of mankind,
items.secondaryText: مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ

items.text: "The Ilah (God) of mankind,
items.secondaryText: إِلَٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ

items.text: "From the evil of the whisperer (devil who whispers evil in the hearts of men) who withdraws (from his whispering in one's heart after one remembers Allah),
items.secondaryText: مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ

items.text: "Who whispers in the breasts of mankind,
items.secondaryText: ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ

items.text: "Of jinns and men."
items.secondaryText: مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ
